summary: [4.5] Editor: the first tab break inside text exactly on the tabstop is invisible

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+)

### Issue description

In the GDScript editor, hitting _tab_ usually inserts a visible tab.
However, if the tab comes exactly on a tab stop (ie, at a multiple of four characters), and sits inside a word, then that tab is not displayed.

This can lead to some really confusing editor messages as GDScript can't parse the hidden whitespace. This can crop up a fair amount if the user's used to tab completion and hits tab too soon, and in the wrong spot.



### Steps to reproduce

Open a new GDScript script.

Type this:
```
var hello = "world"
```
Put the cursor before the `o`. Hit tab. The break is essentially invisible: if you're looking for it, the `o` moves by a pixel or two.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

n/a

summary: Editor hang in RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::fence_wait(), Win11, NVidia, Forward+

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.x, 4.5 stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7260) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads) - 63.71 GiB memory

### Issue description

The editor randomly hangs, and if examined in the debugger, the top stack frame in the main thread is in `RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::fence_wait()` [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/d7382aa05854df37c2f21aff02a693f8104fb95d/drivers/vulkan/rendering_device_driver_vulkan.cpp#L2549). The only solution is to kill the process and restart the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

I have not found a reliable way to trigger it, but when it does trigger, it is almost always immediately after opening or closing a popup window (e.g. color picker, rename text field).

This happens once every a few hours on average, but does not require the editor to have been running for any specific length of time - some days it doesn't happen at all, other days it happens every few minutes.

It may be relevant that I always have editor windows (main window, Inspector, FileSystem, etc.) on different monitors. I don't think it's relevant, but I only use Godot for 2D projects.

If relevant, I have G-SYNC enabled and v-sync set to "Use the 3D application setting" in the NVidia control panel.

I'm happy to investigate further if you have suggestions.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I was able to trigger it at least once in an empty project in Godot 4.5 (New Project, no scenes, no resources) with default settings.

summary: Editor: Implement 'Create Camera from View' action in editor

description.text:
### Summary

Adds a new editor action **Create Camera from View** to **View** menu that instantiates a `Camera3D` node using the current editor viewport's transform, field of view, near and far settings.

### What it does?
- Creates a new `Camera3D` node based on the editor camera
- Preserves transform, FOV and near/far clipping planes
- Adds the camera to the scene tree with undo/redo support
- Automatically selects the new camera in the editor

### Motivation
This complements the existing **Align Transform with View** action by providing a faster, more complete workflow for camera creation. It is especially useful for level design, cinematics and scene layout.

summary: Visible Ratio on RichTextLabel doesn't update size dynamically in Godot 4.5

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

In Godot 4.5, changing visible_ratio in a RichTextLabel with fit_content=true does not dynamically adjust the node's size (neither minimum_size nor the rendered size), unlike Godot 4.4.1, where the box resized in real-time according to the visible text.

This is noticeable when a texture appears expanded to the final size of the text, even before the text has been fully displayed.

Here you have a couple of version comparisons.

Godot 4.4.1
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87a924f0-8585-4684-8d0d-554c8850960c

Godot 4.5
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/68ab328a-077f-456d-ba1e-43d8ef008fe1

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a RichTextLabel with fit_content enabled.

2. Assign multi-line text.

3. Use a tween or script to change visible_ratio from 0.0 to 1.0.

4. Observe that the size does not adjust to the visible text (it remains the total content size).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I’m sharing a test project that includes the features where this bug is reproduced. As you will notice, when manually changing the visible_ratio, the text box consistently retains the final size of the RichTextLabel, as it does not adjust dynamically.

summary: [WIP Draft] The HDR mega-PR

description.text:
This is a combination of the following PRs, merged in no particular order, for the purpose of testing how these PRs work together:

- #94496
- #106940
- ~~#106814~~ (coming soon; I need re-cherrypick this)
- #102987

# Usage

Download the editor build artifact from the [Checks page](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110701/checks) of this PR.

**Note:** Mac OS builds fine if you build it yourself, but fails on Checks, so no build artifact is made.

This PR only supports HDR on Windows, Wayland, and Mac OS for now. SDR testing on other platforms is welcome!

## Windows

Usage on Windows is the similar to #94496 (the difference is now AgX tonemapper supports HDR):

1. Set HDR 2D to enabled to ensure consistent 2D rendering between SDR and HDR modes
2. Change rendering driver to d3d12 to avoid vulkan GPU driver issues on Windows
3. Change tonemapper to Linear, Reinhard, or AgX (Filmic and ACES cannot work in HDR)
4. Set `display/window/request_hdr` to enabled!

## Mac OS

Usage on Mac OS has its own quirks as this is a very early rough prototype:

1. Build Mac OS editor locally
2. Set HDR 2D to enabled to ensure consistent 2D rendering between SDR and HDR modes
3. Disable "Embed game on next play" under the Game workspace (Embedded windows are currently not supported!)
4. Change tonemapper to Linear, Reinhard, or AgX (Filmic and ACES cannot work in HDR)
5. Set `display/window/request_hdr` to enabled!
6. Note: Sometimes Godot will think that HDR output is supported when it is not.

## Linux (Wayland)

1. Enable the wayland server within Godot
2. Set HDR 2D to enabled to ensure consistent 2D rendering between SDR and HDR modes
3. Change tonemapper to Linear, Reinhard, or AgX (Filmic and ACES cannot work in HDR)
4. Set `display/window/request_hdr` to enabled!

## Player settings (max luminance and brightness)

Brightness on Windows is controlled by the OS HDR display settings (“SDR content brightness” or “HDR content brightness”). Brightness on Mac OS is controlled also controlled by the brightness display settings.

Max luminance, used by tonemapping and glow effects, is read from screen information or can be overridden by the player (on Windows only currently) by using the “player facing HDR settings” found in [this demo project](https://github.com/allenwp/godot-hdr-output-test-project). You can also adjust this using the project settings by disabling the  “auto-adjust luminance” settings.

# Things to try

Please give this "mega-PR" a try, especially relating to all combinations the following which have been changed:
- Does HDR mode look the same as SDR mode, but with brighter highlights? (It should!)
- Glow (especially the Screen blend mode, which is now influenced by both tonemapper `white` and HDR output max value)
- AgX tonemapping
- HDR output with the Mobile renderer
- Colour adjustments

# Performance

Optimizations have not been included in this PR and there is room for improvement. Testing performance would be better done on #94496.

summary: Add support for system window menu popup

description.text:
The system window popup menu has been implemented. For applications with custom title bars this can be especially useful, to act similarly to real system bars.

summary: Godot 4.5 NavigationRegion2D/NavigationAgent2D Path finding doesn't work anymore

description.text: ### Tested versions reproduceable in 4.5 NOT reproduceable in 4.4.1 (it's working there) ### System information Windows 11 godot-4.5.stable ### Issue description Since the Update of Godot 4.5 the path finding in a NavigationRegion2D using a NavigationAgent2D doesn't work anymore (in Godot 4.4.1 it worked just seamlessly). Now when i bake the NavMesh in Godot for my NavigationRegion2D i get the following error: ` ERROR: Navigation region synchronization error. More than 2 edges tried to occupy the same map rasterization space. This is a logical error in the navigation mesh caused by overlap or too densely placed edges.` Now i have already seen a similar bug report from a 4.5 Beta i guess and someone said, that they changed the order of some checks in the 2D Navigation Code so i am aware that these order changes took place in order to achieve the independet 2D Navigation (which now isn't dependent from the 3D Navigation anymore). But still it would be really good to update the 2D Navigation Code so that it is working just the same as before in Godot 4.4.1 while still keeping it independent from the 3D Navigation. Since Google blocks all app/game updates which are not using 16KB page size starting with November 1st, it would be extremely important to address and fix this issue as soon as possible, since all developers that have issues with 4.5 will not be able to update their app/game anymore.. I already asked the Godot Forum for help and people told me the path finding should just work without changing anything at all, which it didn't, so these people told me it must be a bug and i should report it (even thought someone else already did it before in the Beta -> but it didn't get fixed) I hope you guys understand the importance of this issue.. I also attached a Screenshot of my NavMesh so you can see there are no overlapps or anything like that. I have seen people with simple Path findings that work, but i think on more complex NavigationRegion2D's the issue is almost guaranteed to happen. I think the Demo Program does actually work so i would not take it as reference. summary: GDScript Editor auto-completion suggestions does not update after erasing trailing dot access

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Linux Mint 22.2 (Zara) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (TGL GT2) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1135G7 @ 2.40GHz (8 threads) - 7.40 GiB memory

### Issue description

When typing an expression in the Editor that involves accessing the property of an object, erasing the property when its ~4 characters long seems to incorrectly continue to show auto-complete suggestions for the previously accessed property. Example: ```get_tree().root```

*Expected*: ```get_tree().``` suggestions should show SceneTree properties after deleting the trailing ```root.```.
*Actual*: ```get_tree().``` suggestions still show Window related properties even after erasing the trailing ```root.```.

Additional Info:
- I'm using the built-in Script Editor with no plugins or extentions.
- 100% reliability of reproduction
- Attached: Demo of described issue in action

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b92352aa-88a8-4606-b728-3e6a6ce03c77)

### Steps to reproduce

- Open any GDScript file.
- Write an expression that accesses another property with a short name (example: ```get_tree().root```). Auto-completion shows Window properties/methods as intended.
- Erase the property after the dot operator (example: remove 'root' from ```get_tree().root```, leaving ```get_tree().```) Using either the Backspace Key (spamming or holding down), or selecting the text using Shift + Arrows, then Backspace. Auto-completion still shows Window properties/methods even tho a SceneTree is now being accessed.

summary: Save All Scenes shortcut frequently does not work

description.text:
### Tested versions

Found in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], not present in 4.4 official. I consider this a UX side regression.

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7688) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900KF CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

There are a number of primary symptoms in the 4.5 update affecting saving within the editor. Most of these stem to a change to the "save all scenes" shortcut.

1. Always: Shortcuts relating to saving scenes, including "save scene" and "save all scenes" will not trigger.
2. Rarely: The (*) in the window title will persist even after all scripts and scenes have been saved. Cause unknown.
3. Possibly: Many saving operations no longer cause a progress bar to appear. There is no feedback to whether or not a save has occurred.

I want to focus this issue on point number 1, as it is the root cause in why the editor feels different now. And it's preventing me from assessing if 2 and 3 are *actually* different now, or if its just a symptom of 1.

**Apparent behavior in 4.5**

* The "save all scenes" shortcut now requires the editor to be in a "scene editing" state to be able to trigger.
* This requires focusing the Scene dock, or interacting with a Scene in the Main view.
* This shortcut **sometimes does not work** even when the Main view is displaying a 2D/3D editor.
* It appears to only save scenes now. (Perhaps, due to it not saving all resources anymore, that is why the (*) is not clearing?)

**Apparent behavior in 4.4**

* The "save all scenes" shortcut would execute regardless of the open view.
* It would execute regardless of if any scenes actually need saving. Defined by the saving progress bar popup appearing for a split second, even when nothing was saved.
* It would function in conjunction with the script editor. Saving all open scripts, and saving all open scenes, and saving all edited resources in the inspector history.

**Attempted Remedies**
* Rebinding "save all scenes" to ctrl+s from its default (I have done this in every Godot version). This does not affect the behavior.
* Removing conflicting shortcuts, so that only this one shortcut is triggered by ctrl+s. This does not affect the behavior.

**Proposed fix**
Either, once again make she shortcut independent of the current editor focus.
Or, allow setting shortcuts as "global" so individual shortcuts can ignore the editor focus.
Or, add a "save absolutely everything everywhere" shortcut! :D

### Steps to reproduce

1. Edit a scene
2. Move to the script editor
3. Use the "save all scenes" shortcut (not from the menu, but actually hit the shortcut)
4. Nothing happens | Previous this would save all scripts, scenes, and resources.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

-

summary: If the game window is embedded on macOS, disabling accumulated input causes just_pressed and just_released functions to never return true

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested on:
- v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]
- v4.5.stable.mono.official [876b29033]

Tested with both GDScript and C# scripts; uploaded a GDScript repro project

(Did not test earlier versions, as macOS embedded play is new to 4.5)

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Max (Apple7) - Apple M1 Max (10 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

I am running Godot 4.5 on macOS, with the game window embedded (i.e. "Embed Game on Next Play" is checked).

If I have a script call `Input.set_use_accumulated_input(false)`, then `Input.is_action_just_pressed(...)` and `Input.is_action_just_released(...)` will always return false. However, `Input.is_action_pressed(...)` does seem to work correctly.

Removing the call to `Input.set_use_accumulated_input(false)` "fixes" the issue. Additionally, unchecking "Embed Game on Next Play" will also cause the issue to go away.

### Steps to reproduce

(N.B. I've attached a minimal repro project that includes a scene with the node/script mentioned)

1. Open a project in Godot 4.5 on macOS
2. In the "Game" tab, under the embedding options (far right "snowman" menu with three dots), ensure that "Embed Game on Next Play" is checked
3. Run the game, with the following script attached to a node in the scene:
```gdscript
extends Node

func _init() -> void:
	Input.set_use_accumulated_input(false)

func _process(delta: float):
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_accept"):
		print("Accept just pressed")
	
	if Input.is_action_pressed("ui_accept"):
		print("Accept is pressed")
		
	if Input.is_action_just_released("ui_accept"):
		print("Accept just released")
```
4. Press the Enter key on your keyboard

**Observed Behavior:**
The Output log looks like the following:
```
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
```

**Expected Behavior:**
The log should instead look like the following:
```
Accept just pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept just released
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[input_issue_demo_2025-09-17_17-09-59.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22396349/input_issue_demo_2025-09-17_17-09-59.zip)

However, if running a HTML deploy for the browser, two events actually fire and the second event is always 0, making the final pressure value at the end of a frame be 0, essentially not detecting pressure on browser. Windows behavior, pressure being read correctly: Browser behavior, pressure is 0.0 every other event in each frame: I thought this could be my 120hz monitor being funny but limiting the fps to 30 or even 10 doesn't remove the issue. Another observation I don't know if should be a separate bug or what: > Mouse pressure with a normal mouse is 0.5 on browser but 1.0 on windows, which is inconsistent behavior and, as it's impossible to tell if the input is from a mouse or tablet, almost impossible to work around. I could get my pen tablet to print exactly 0.5 pressure so I can't just == compare that. ### Steps to reproduce make a html browser export template create an _input(event) function if event is InputEventMouseMotion, save the pressure to a variable use the variable somewhere after all inputs have been processed (say, in _process) check that value in a browser deploy ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [pen-pressure-browser.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22394874/pen-pressure-browser.zip) EDIT: Grammar

summary: CompositorEffect not getting accurate stencil buffer unless I switch effect_callback_type off of Post-Transparent, then back

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproduces on 4.5-stable ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sonoma (14.7.8) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory ### Issue description I have a CompositorEffect that draws outlines based on values in the stencil buffer. Even though the stencil values appear to be correct (checking in a debug quad with a stencil-read shader), the outlines are not drawn when the process starts. Here's where it gets weird, if I switch the CompositorEffect callback type from Post-Transparent, to Pre-Transparent, then back to Post-Transparent, it begins to work. In the attached video, you'll see this, along with trying to just disable/enable the CompositorEffect to get it to work. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cef35052-b9bb-4785-906b-8a1d3b12ce70 One additional note, if I switch the orange StandardMaterial in the MRP to write the stencil itself, the outlines work immediately. It looks like it may be related to using a dumb stencil-write ShaderMaterial, then a StandardMaterial as the next_pass. Dumb shader for writing to stencil buffer: ```gdshader shader_type spatial; render_mode blend_mix; stencil_mode write, compare_always, 1; void fragment() { ALPHA = 0.0; } ``` ### Steps to reproduce * Start the scene in the MRP * Note that there are no yellow outlines * Toggle the DebugStencilQuad to see where the outlines should be drawn * In the Camera3D, under Compositor, in the first Compositor, `StencilBasedOutine...` change "Enabled" to false, then true * Nothing will change on screen * In the Compositor settings "Effect Callback Type" from "Post Transparent" to "Pre Transparent" * Nothing will change on screen * Change the "Effect Callback Type" back to "Post Transparent" * Outlines will now be shown on screen Location of CompositorEffect settings because they're deep in there: . ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [next_pass-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22393349/next_pass-issue.zip)

summary: [4.5] Cannot edit values of Resources of Instanced Scenes until making it unique

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested on v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]
Was behaving differently in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (RADV NAVI22) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

When I instance a new scene of an already existing object, and this object has an exported Resource that is not filled, when I fill this variable with a resource, I cannot edit any of the values of the resource until I explicitly make it unique.

For example, I have a Room, and I add a Box to it. 
The Box needs to have a Resource (exported variable), but the content can be one of many different Resources, hence it is not set to be one specific one.
In the Room scene that I have added the Box to, I am now adding a Resource (of type Money).

In the previous version of Godot (4.4), I could immediately edit the values of the Money resource.
In 4.5, I fist have to make the Money resource unique.

If the new behavior is intended, it is, in my opinion, just an additional step with no clear benefit. That said, I could not see anything specifically talking about this behavior in the Changelog for 4.5

### Steps to reproduce

- Have a scene (Room)
- add an instanced scene (Box) to Room, that can hold a resource, with no resource being set inside of the Box scene itself.
- Add a Resource to the Box node inside of the Room scene
- you cannot edit any of the values of the added resource until you make the resource unique.

Arch Linux, Gnome DE - v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] - Vulkan 1.4.321 (Forward+) - 11th Gen Intel i3-1115G4 (4) @ 4.100GHz 

### Issue description

If you try to change the text of the Label node through a custom function by calling this function in another node, making the node where this function is in another node global, then the old text will remain along with the new one.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a main control node scene with a button as a child
2. Make another control scene but with label in it(also make sure that there is text in label)
3. Make that one scene with label global
4. Put scene with label in scene with button
5. Create script attached to the scene with label
6. In that script write code with custom function that changes text property of label to something else
7. Create script attached to the button
8. Connect signal "pressed" to custom function that calls custom function in scene with label
9. Now when you press button the new text appear on top of label, while old text is still here

summary: Baked LightmapGI invisble after baking it a second time in the scene until reopening scene (Godot 4.5)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in 4.5
Not reproducable in 4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8700 CPU @ 3.20GHz (12 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

I noticed that after I baked the lightmap a second time everything stayed dark.
Then I first tried it in Godot 4.4.1, where everything worked as expected. Back to Godot 4.5, I tried reloading the scene after the bake and suddenly the previously new baked lightmap appeared

### Steps to reproduce

Baking the lightmap the first time should work as intended. As this scene contains a baked csg mesh, delete this, assign a different material to the emissive object and rebake the csg mesh.
Then try to rebake the lightmap, In godot 4.5 it will look dark like if it didn't bake, while in 4.4.1 it bakes as it should.
Finally save the scene, close and open again, the baked lighting will apear now.
